verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p24delta1) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein belonging to the p24/TMED family. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is localized to both the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae, confirming a documented dual-localization between an intracellular (Golgi/ERGIC) compartment and the plasma membrane. The protein cycles between these compartments as part of its role in COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking. While the Golgi is the dominant functional compartment for TMED10 in most contexts, a genuine PM pool exists and has been documented. This pattern—cycling between Golgi and PM with a minority surface pool—fits dual_localization under contextual. The protein has its own TM anchor at the surface during its PM residency stage. No specific cell-death, activation-induced, germline-restricted, or lysosomal-exocytosis surface story is required here; the documented steady-state PM pool plus vesicular cycling is sufficient for contextual/dual_localization.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The relative proportion of PM vs. Golgi pool for TMED10 is not well-quantified; surface expression may be minor and context-dependent.
